name: Kai Winding
description: Danish-born American jazz composer and trombonist
abstract: Kai Chresten Winding ( KY WIN-ding; May 18, 1922 – May 6, 1983) was an American trombonist and jazz composer. He is known for his collaborations with fellow trombonist J.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Monk

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Kai and I did get to team up and form a partnership. It was a partnership in the classic sense that we split up all responsibilities, all income, all everything evenly down the middle.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: And then I did two weeks with the Kai Winding group... In the group was Zoot Sims, myself, Joe Newman, Billy Taylor, Kai Winding was the leader...
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: So with me when I went in this place I had Oscar Pettiford, Kenny Clarke on drums, Kenny Durham on trumpet, and Kai Winding on trombone.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Katz
about_quotes.evidence: So after Tony Scott I started working with Kai Winding and J.J. Johnson... And I made about six albums with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: I got hired for the gig... Bill worked in Kai Winding’s group from 1962-67
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Harris... recommended me to Kai for his group. So that’s when Kai called me... Kai is going to do a record... I wrote this piece called... 'Nutcracker.' And Kai liked it. Recorded it on the first record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002
